title: Processes of a supranational regulatory authority
challenge: Complex regulatory requirements had to be translated into implementable digital processes while reducing the risk of acceptance-test findings.
actions: Extracted mandatory scenarios, roles and constraints from regulations so the delivery team could work with unambiguous requirements.
Aligned target processes with stakeholders and resolved contradictions before acceptance testing.
Designed the solution and supported development to preserve regulatory and business compliance.
result: Two processes passed acceptance testing on the first attempt, reducing the risk of repeated testing, additional rework and launch delays.

title: Machine-readable powers of attorney
challenge: New legislation had to be incorporated into an existing product quickly without breaking established user workflows.
actions: Translated legal requirements into clear product rules and constraints.
Interviewed users to validate real-world power-of-attorney workflows.
Defined and validated target scenarios, reducing the risk of building an inconvenient or unnecessary feature.
result: The functionality reached production, enabling the product to comply with new legislation on time while preserving practical user workflows.

title: Contact centre monitoring
challenge: Managers lacked a consolidated view of chat and messenger interactions, making workload and performance difficult to assess.
actions: Defined the management metrics needed to control digital channels.
Designed a data model and processing logic that brought fragmented events into a consistent structure.
Built SQL logic and Grafana dashboards that turned technical data into clear management indicators.
result: Managers gained transparent analytics for chats and messengers, creating a stronger basis for workload planning and operational decisions.

title: Operational reporting modernisation
challenge: Manual Excel reporting required repetitive work, did not scale and made the outcome overly dependent on individual employees.
actions: Clarified which indicators stakeholders actually used for decisions.
Introduced centralised storage and automated data processing instead of manually assembled files.
Moved visualisation to Power BI and Qlik Sense to make recurring analysis easier and more accessible.
result: Reporting moved to a reliable database-and-BI setup, reducing manual work, improving calculation consistency and simplifying access to current metrics.

title: Report builder
challenge: Contact centre administrators depended on developers whenever a new report or dashboard was needed.
actions: Researched common administrator tasks and identified the parameters they genuinely needed to control.
Designed an intuitive self-service report configuration flow that required no technical expertise.
Defined interface rules and safeguards that balanced flexibility with configuration reliability.
result: Administrators could create dashboards independently, reducing developer dependency, accelerating access to new reporting and freeing the delivery team for product work.
